title: Tutorial 4: commit does not work as expected
body:
**Description**

I am working on a project that uses buildah as a library. The [tutorial 4](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/docs/tutorials/04-include-in-your-build-tool.md) does not work as expected. The line:
```
imageRef, err := alltransports.ParseImageName("docker.io/myusername/my-image")
```
today will raise an error. Actually, the function `func ParseImageName(imgName string) (types.ImageReference, error)` expects a string respecting an URL format like `docker://localhost/my-image`.

As a result, `buildah images` returns neither repository nor tag:
```
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
<none>                     <none>   7d063adc4f98   18 hours ago   103 MB
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Copy and paste the code given at the end of the tutorial 4 in a go file
2. Run it `go run main.go`.
3. Issue `buildah images`


**Describe the results you received:**
Step 2 looks like everything went ok:
```
Image built! 92876e9ba3b33642c1908fbb55661135a92bdf5dfa415ea2130d29e9320d96af
```
But `buildah images` will show the image has no name nor any tag.
```
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
<none>                     <none>   92876e9ba3b3   23 seconds ago   201 MB
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
I think the result should have been:
```
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
docker.io/myusername/my-image latest   92876e9ba3b3   23 seconds ago   201 MB
```
**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
apt list buildah                                                                                                                                                                   
Listing... Done
buildah/bionic,now 1.10.1-1~ubuntu18.04~ppa1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.10.1
Go Version:      go1.10.4
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.1-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
Git Commit:
Built:           Thu Aug  8 20:29:48 2019
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=18.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=bionic
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.3 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS"
VERSION_ID="18.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=bionic
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux docker-for-laurent 4.15.0-72-generic #81-Ubuntu SMP Tue Nov 26 12:20:02 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body: Since the PR is merged, this is no longer an issue.
